name: Eta HTTP/2 server tail latency over real sockets

description: Baseline: H2 tail latency over real h2c sockets (oha, 1 conn/16 streams, n=40000, median of 3). p99 geomean 4639µs; static_1k/echo_1k tails (6959/7955µs) dominate vs root/user_id (2628/3184µs).

asi.hypothesis: baseline of H2 tail latency over real sockets
asi.tail_profile: p99 geomean 4639us. Body-bearing endpoints dominate the tail: static_1k p99 6959us, echo_1k p99 7955us, vs root 2628us / user_id 3184us. p99/p50 ratio: root 2.05x, user_id 2.22x, static_1k 3.00x, echo_1k 3.30x -- the body path has the worst tail amplification.
asi.rps_consistency: rps_geomean 113264 matches throughput session 114091 -- harness consistent
asi.next: profile what spikes on the body path (static_1k/echo_1k). Prime suspects: minor GC pauses from per-request allocation (DATA frame body copy, HPACK string alloc), and the handler-timeout watchdog timer wakeups. First cheap experiment: GC tuning at probe startup (larger minor heap to cut collection frequency) -- measure tail + RSS tradeoff.

description: h2_response: single-chunk fixed-body fast path avoids redundant Bytes.concat full-body copy (cuts ~1KB alloc/req on body endpoints)

asi.hypothesis: single-chunk fixed responses copy the whole body via Bytes.concat Bytes.empty [chunk] (fresh alloc + memcpy even for one element); reinterpret the lone chunk in place to cut ~1KB alloc/req on body endpoints
asi.result: p99 geomean 4639->4549 (-1.9%). RSS 113408->109588kb (-3.4%, confirms real allocation reduction). rps_geomean 113264->113767 (flat/up). static_1k p99 6959->5219 (single-chunk file, direct beneficiary). echo_1k p99 7955->8915 (regressed -- p99 noise; echo also single-chunk and should benefit).
asi.noise_warning: p99 is very noisy across runs; per-endpoint values swing >1ms. geomean improvement is small (90us). Need 3+ runs for confidence. The allocation cut itself is unambiguous (RSS down).
asi.validation: all H2 unit suites pass (checks 3.6s). Safe: the lone chunk is a freshly produced, unaliased response body owned by the response after the handler returns.
asi.next: Confirm with a re-run, OR pursue a broader allocation cut. Next candidates: h2_response builds a new normalized header list (List.map normalize_name) per response; add_content_length_header does string_of_int per response; per-command promise allocation in await_owner. The tail driver is minor-GC frequency -- need to cut total words/req, not just body copy.
